channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - www.gamedeveloper.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for www.gamedeveloper.com

item.title: Time Games' opening lineup includes a fantasy prediction market game
item.content:encoded:
Time has launched its own assortment of daily digital games, which include fantasy market predictions powered by real-time data.

In a similar fashion to The New York Times, Time Games is the latest example of a publisher venturing into the video game business. Officially introduced yesterday, following a report from Axios, Time Games is free-to-play and optimized for both mobile and desktop.

The assortment includes classic games like Sudoku and Word Search, as well as a few proprietary ones. Market Movers, for example, is a fantasy prediction market game powered by real-time data, where players run predictions of current events and markets.

Time calls Market Movers a "first-of-its-kind." While the game doesn't involve real-world money, the timing matches an ongoing controversy around actual prediction markets, which are increasingly deemed problematic across the globe. The likes of Polymarket, for example, have been scrutinized for their reported role in shaping news, as users are able to buy and sell shares linked to the probability of real-world events occurring. At the beginning of the month, Time itself reported that, days after the Senate banned its members and staffers from trading on prediction markets, Senate minority leader Chuck Schumer called on the Trump administration to follow suit.

Related:Update: Microsoft Israel's general manager set to leave amid alleged unethical use of Azure

Aside from Market Movers, there's also Linked, a daily word-connection puzzle that has users connecting seemingly unrelated words in a few moves, and Word Flower, a "petal-based" word game that has players building words from a central cluster of letters.

Notably, Time Games also includes Time-related experiences. What Year Was It? has folks guessing a year through Time's cover in a timed quiz. Meanwhile, the Time Cover Jigsaw offers exactly what it says on the tin.

"Long before the internet, Time was pioneering interactive formats — from news quizzes to games — that gave readers a reason to engage beyond the headlines," Time chief operating officer Mark Howard said in the announcement. "Time Games is a natural evolution of that spirit: building a daily habit with our audience, meeting them where they already are, and giving them something personal, playful, and distinctly Time to return to."

Time Games is supported by Lucky Butterfly Productions, Inc., which is in charge of Linked, and Amuse Labs, which provides the technology for Time Cover Jigsaw, Word Search, Word Flower, and Sudoku.

Digital newspaper games have carved their own niche in the game business for a couple of years now. 2022 was marked by the notable acquisition of Wordle by the New York Times Company for a sum "in the low seven figures." According to the newspaper at the time, Wordle's player base surged from 300,000 to millions over the course of January in that year.

Related:Last Flag dev Night Street Games is laying off staff

As we reported in late 2024, publications and platforms definitely took notice of Wordle's popularity—LinkedIn launched three "thinking-oriented games," Vulture unveiled the grid-based movie trivia guessing game Cinematrix, and the subscription-based games platform Puzzmo was around in 2023. Puzzmo was then acquired by Hearst Newspapers, making it playable on websites like the San Francisco Chronicle.

About the Author

Contributing Editor, News, GameDeveloper.com

Diego Nicolás Argüello is a freelance journalist and critic from Argentina. Video games helped him to learn English, so now he covers them for places like The New York Times, NPR, Rolling Stone, and more. He also runs Into the Spine, a site dedicated to fostering and supporting new writers, and co-hosted Turnabout Breakdown, a podcast about the Ace Attorney series. He’s most likely playing a rhythm game as you read this.



https://www.gamedeveloper.com/business/time-launches-its-own-nyt-games-equivalent
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
